annotations.label: Continuum hypothesis

anchor.snippet: There is no set whose cardinality is strictly between that of the integers and the real numbers

annotations.note: Mathlib has the provable direction ℵ₁ ≤ 𝔠 but never defines or asserts CH itself (which is independent of ZFC).

annotations.label: CH equivalent in aleph/beth numbers

anchor.snippet: this is equivalent to the following equation in aleph numbers

annotations.note: Mathlib has 𝔠 = 2^ℵ₀ (Cardinal.two_power_aleph0) and ℵ₁ ≤ 𝔠 but not the equation 2^ℵ₀ = ℵ₁.

annotations.label: Generalized continuum hypothesis

anchor.snippet: The generalized continuum hypothesis states that

annotations.note: No GCH definition exists anywhere in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Kőnig's theorem

anchor.snippet: established Kőnig's theorem , which by using the concept of cofinality

annotations.note: Cardinal.sum_lt_prod is explicitly labelled König's theorem (∀i, f i < g i → sum f < prod g).

annotations.label: Same cardinality

anchor.snippet: Two sets are said to have the same cardinality or cardinal number if there exists a bijection

annotations.note: Cardinal.eq states #α = #β ↔ Nonempty (α ≃ β), i.e. equal cardinality iff a bijection exists.

annotations.label: Rationals are countable

anchor.snippet: the rational numbers can actually be placed in one-to-one correspondence with the integers

annotations.note: Cardinal.mkRat proves #ℚ = ℵ₀ = #ℤ (Cardinal.mk_int), giving the bijection with the integers.

annotations.label: Integers strictly smaller than reals

anchor.snippet: Cantor gave two proofs that the cardinality of the set of integers is strictly smaller than that of the set of real numbers

annotations.note: Cardinal.aleph0_lt_continuum (ℵ₀ < 𝔠) with Cardinal.mk_int (#ℤ=ℵ₀) and Cardinal.mk_real (#ℝ=𝔠) gives #ℤ < #ℝ.

annotations.label: Unique smallest cardinal greater than aleph-null

anchor.snippet: there is a unique smallest cardinal number

annotations.note: ℵ₁ = succ ℵ₀ (Cardinal.succ_aleph0) and Cardinal.aleph_one_le_iff (ℵ₁ ≤ c ↔ ℵ₀ < c) express that ℵ₁ is the least cardinal exceeding ℵ₀.

annotations.label: Continuum Hypothesis (formal)
annotations.note: Only the provable inequality ℵ₁ ≤ 𝔠 is present; the CH equality 2^ℵ₀ = ℵ₁ is independent of ZFC and not stated.

annotations.label: Gödel: CH not disprovable in ZF

anchor.snippet: showed that CH cannot be disproved from ZF

annotations.note: Mathlib has no metatheory of ZF(C) consistency or constructible-universe relative consistency results.

annotations.label: Cohen: CH not provable in ZFC

anchor.snippet: showed that CH cannot be proven from the ZFC axioms

annotations.note: Mathlib has no forcing machinery or independence results for ZFC.

annotations.label: Solovay's forcing result on the continuum

anchor.snippet: then there is a forcing extension in which

annotations.note: No forcing or model-of-ZFC infrastructure exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Freiling's axiom of symmetry equivalent to ¬CH

anchor.section: Arguments for and against the continuum hypothesis
anchor.snippet: the negation of CH is equivalent to Freiling's axiom of symmetry

annotations.note: Freiling's axiom of symmetry and its equivalence with ¬CH are absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: Star axiom falsifies CH

anchor.section: Arguments for and against the continuum hypothesis
anchor.snippet: The Star axiom would imply that

annotations.note: The Star axiom (Woodin) and its consequences are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Generalized continuum hypothesis (full statement)

anchor.snippet: if an infinite set's cardinality lies between that of an infinite set S and that of the power set

annotations.note: No GCH definition (no cardinal strictly between #S and #𝒫S) exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: GCH in beth numbers

anchor.snippet: The beth numbers provide an alternative notation for this condition

annotations.note: Beth numbers (Cardinal.beth) and aleph numbers are defined, but the GCH equivalence ℵ_α = ℶ_α is not stated.

annotations.label: Sierpiński: ZF + GCH implies AC

anchor.snippet: Sierpiński proved that ZF + GCH implies the axiom of choice

annotations.note: Neither GCH nor the ZF-relative derivation of choice from it is formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Gödel: GCH consistent via V=L

anchor.snippet: Kurt Gödel showed that GCH is a consequence of ZF + V=L

annotations.note: Mathlib has no constructible universe (V=L) or GCH consistency development.

annotations.label: Easton's theorem

anchor.snippet: to prove Easton's theorem , which shows it is consistent with ZFC

annotations.note: Easton forcing and its consistency conclusions are not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Foreman–Woodin consistency result

anchor.snippet: Foreman and Woodin proved that

annotations.note: This forcing-based consistency result is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Merimovich consistency result

anchor.snippet: Carmi Merimovich [ 33 ] showed that, for each n ≥ 1 , it is consistent with ZFC

annotations.note: This forcing-based consistency result is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Patai's theorem on successor exponentiation

anchor.snippet: László Patai [ 34 ] proved that if γ is an ordinal

annotations.note: Patai's result on cardinal successor exponentiation is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Injection lifts to subsets

anchor.snippet: if there is an injection from A to B then there is an injection from subsets of A to subsets of B

annotations.note: With Cardinal.mk_powerset (#𝒫s = 2^#s), Cardinal.power_le_power_left gives #A ≤ #B → 2^#A ≤ 2^#B, the powerset monotonicity claimed.

annotations.label: GCH determines cardinal exponentiation

anchor.section: Implications of GCH for cardinal exponentiation
anchor.snippet: GCH implies that for ordinals α and β

annotations.note: GCH is undefined in Mathlib, so its computation of cardinal exponentiation is absent.
